items.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger` **Service directory:** `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/` **Triggered by:** commit d543f21a7eec10a86152e94f2d19636fb13fcd01 by `@ryazhang-microsoft` ## What Changed PR #57869 significantly refactored `ConfidentialLedgerRedirectPolicy` to cache the primary node URL from redirect responses and reuse it for subsequent non-GET requests. This improves write operation performance on multi-node ACL clusters. The policy was originally introduced in 1.4.1-beta.3 and improved in 1.4.1-beta.5. Neither the original introduction nor the caching improvement is reflected in the README. ## Gaps Found - **`## Examples` section says "Coming Soon..."** — despite the `Key concepts` section already containing inline code snippets, there is no organized Examples section with runnable, snippet-backed samples - **`ConfidentialLedgerRedirectPolicy` is undocumented** — the automatic 307/308 redirect following (with Authorization header preservation) is a significant behavioral feature that users migrating from earlier versions or other SDKs need to know about; no README section explains this - **`VerifyConnection` default change is undocumented** — beta.4 changed the default for `VerifyConnection` in `ConfidentialLedgerClientOptions` from `false` to `true`; this breaking-by-intent change has no README guidance - **API reference documentation link is commented out** — `` is commented out in the header badge row
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/README.md` — **edit** #### 1a. Add a "Redirect handling" subsection under `Key concepts` Insert the following after the `### Confidential consortium and enclave verifications` section and before `### Thread safety`: ```markdown ### Redirect handling Azure Confidential Ledger is a distributed system with multiple nodes. Write operations (non-GET requests) must be routed to the primary node; a non-primary node returns an HTTP 307/308 redirect to the current primary. The SDK automatically handles these redirects while preserving the `Authorization` header (which standard HTTP clients strip on cross-domain redirects). For efficiency, the SDK caches the most recently observed primary node URL and uses it as the first target for subsequent write requests, reducing the number of redirects experienced during steady-state operation. The cache is refreshed automatically whenever the service redirects to a new primary node. This behavior is built into `ConfidentialLedgerRedirectPolicy`, which is added to the pipeline by default. No extra configuration is required. ``` #### 1b. Replace the `## Examples` placeholder Replace: ```markdown ## Examples Coming Soon... ``` With: ```markdown ## Examples ### Append an entry and verify it was committed ```C# Snippet:AppendToLedger Operation postOperation = ledgerClient.PostLedgerEntry( waitUntil: WaitUntil.Completed, RequestContent.Create( new { contents = "Hello world!" })); string transactionId = postOperation.Id; Console.WriteLine($"Appended transaction with Id: {transactionId}"); ``` ### Check transaction status ```C# Snippet:GetStatus Response statusResponse = ledgerClient.GetTransactionStatus(transactionId); string status = JsonDocument.Parse(statusResponse.Content) .RootElement .GetProperty("state") .GetString(); Console.WriteLine($"Transaction status: {status}"); // Wait for the entry to be committed while (status == "Pending") { statusResponse = ledgerClient.GetTransactionStatus(transactionId); status = JsonDocument.Parse(statusResponse.Content) .RootElement .GetProperty("state") .GetString(); } Console.WriteLine($"Transaction status: {status}"); ``` ### Get a cryptographic receipt ```C# Snippet:GetReceipt Response receiptResponse = ledgerClient.GetReceipt(transactionId); string receiptJson = new StreamReader(receiptResponse.ContentStream).ReadToEnd(); Console.WriteLine(receiptJson); ``` ``` > **Note:** The snippet names (`AppendToLedger`, `GetStatus`, `GetReceipt`) are already defined in the existing Key concepts section. Confirm the snippet test file exists at `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/tests/samples/` — if the `#region Snippet:` tags are present there, the `Update-Snippets.ps1` tool will fill in the code automatically. If not, the snippet regions should be added to the test file. #### 1c. Document `VerifyConnection` default in the "Authenticate the client" section Add under `#### Using Azure Active Directory`: ```markdown > **Note:** By default, `ConfidentialLedgerClientOptions.VerifyConnection` is `true`, which means the client validates the TLS certificate of the ledger node it connects to. Set this to `false` only in test or development environments. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets The snippets `AppendToLedger`, `GetStatus`, `GetReceipt` should already exist in the Key concepts section of the README and in a test/sample file. Verify: 1. Check `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/tests/samples/` for existing `#region Snippet:` tags for `AppendToLedger`, `GetStatus`, and `GetReceipt`. 2. If not present, create `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/tests/Samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs` with the sample code wrapped in `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion` blocks. 3. Add the snippet placeholders in README under `## Examples` as shown above. 4. Run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 confidentialledger` to inject the code. Reference: `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/tests/samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/README.md` must contain these sections in this order. Current state: | Section | Status | |---|---| | Getting started / Install the package | ✅ Present | | Getting started / Prerequisites | ✅ Present | | Getting started / Authenticate the client | ✅ Present (minor gap: no VerifyConnection note) | | Key concepts | ✅ Present (but missing redirect policy) | | Examples | ❌ "Coming Soon..." — needs real content | | Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | | Next steps | ✅ Present | | Contributing | ✅ Present | ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 confidentialledger` — confirms snippets are injected from test files into README 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 confidentialledger` — no new public types were added, but run to confirm API listings are current 5. `dotnet format sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
items.title: [ConfidentialLedger] Docs: Document redirect policy behavior and complete Examples section in README
